diffs.diff:
--- a/explore4.py
+++ b/explore4.py
@@ -0,0 +1,69 @@
+import os, sys

+sys.path.insert(0, '/opt/triposr')

+

+from tsr.utils import get_spherical_cameras, get_intrinsic_from_fov, get_ray_directions, get_rays

+import inspect

+

+# Get full source of get_spherical_cameras

+print('=== get_spherical_cameras ===')

+src = inspect.getsource(get_spherical_cameras)

+for i, line in enumerate(src.split('\n')):

+    print(f'{i:3d}: {line}')

+

+print('\n=== get_intrinsic_from_fov ===')

+src = inspect.getsource(get_intrinsic_from_fov)

+for i, line in enumerate(src.split('\n')):

+    print(f'{i:3d}: {line}')

+

+print('\n=== get_ray_directions ===')

+src = inspect.getsource(get_ray_directions)

+for i, line in enumerate(src.split('\n')):

+    print(f'{i:3d}: {line}')

+

+# Now test TripoSR loading and inference

+print('\n=== Testing TripoSR ===')

+from tsr.system import TSR

+import torch

+

+device = 'cuda:0' if torch.cuda.is_available() else 'cpu'

+print(f'Device: {device}')

+

+model = TSR.from_pretrained(

+    '/models/triposr',

+    config_name='config.yaml',

+    weight_name='model.ckpt',

+)

+model.renderer.set_chunk_size(131072)

+model.to(device)

+print('Model loaded')

+

+# Test on a sample

+from PIL import Image

+data_dir = os.environ.get('RAB_DATA_DIR', '/data')

+train_dir = os.path.join(data_dir, 'train')

+sample = os.listdir(train_dir)[0]

+img_path = os.path.join(train_dir, sample, 'input.png')

+img = Image.open(img_path).convert('RGB')

+print(f'Image size: {img.size}, mode: {img.mode}')

+

+# Run inference

+with torch.no_grad():

+    scene_codes = model([img], device=device)

+print(f'Scene codes shape: {scene_codes.shape if hasattr(scene_codes, "shape") else type(scene_codes)}')

+

+# Test default rendering

+with torch.no_grad():

+    frames = model.render(scene_codes, n_views=11, return_type='pil')

+print(f'Frames type: {type(frames)}')

+print(f'Num objects: {len(frames)}')

+if len(frames) > 0:

+    print(f'Num views per object: {len(frames[0])}')

+    print(f'Frame size: {frames[0][0].size}, mode: {frames[0][0].mode}')

+

+# Check cameras.json for the sample

+import json

+with open(os.path.join(train_dir, sample, 'cameras.json')) as f:

+    cams = json.load(f)

+print(f'\nCameras for sample {sample}:')

+for c in cams:

+    print(f'  view {c["view_id"]}: az={c["azimuth"]:.1f}, el={c["elevation"]:.1f}, dist={c["distance"]:.3f}')
